issue_title: `conda env export` fail
issue_body:
`conda env -h` help (the first output block below) suggests that I should be able to do a simple `export` to output (presumably) my current Conda environment, however when I attempt it (second output block) I get a message suggesting I can't do this and an alternative is proposed, which I then attempt (third output block), and that reports an error and doesn't output anything.

```
➜  notebooks git:(master) ✗ conda env export -h
usage: conda-env export [-h] [-c CHANNEL] [--override-channels] [-n NAME]
                        [-f FILE] [--no-builds] [--ignore-channels]

Export a given environment

Options:

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            Show this help message and exit.
  -c CHANNEL, --channel CHANNEL
                        Additional channel to include in the export
  --override-channels   Do not include .condarc channels
  -n NAME, --name NAME  name of environment (in /Users/ijstokes/anaconda/envs)
  -f FILE, --file FILE
  --no-builds           Remove build specification from dependencies
  --ignore-channels     Do not include channel names with package names.

examples:
    conda env export
    conda env export --file SOME_FILE
```

```
➜  notebooks git:(master) ✗ conda env export --file root.yml


CondaEnvException: Conda Env Exception: Unable to determine environment

Please re-run this command with one of the following options:

* Provide an environment name via --name or -n
* Re-run this command inside an activated conda environment.
```


```
➜  notebooks git:(master) ✗ conda env export -n root --file root.yml
DEPRECATION: The default format will switch to columns in the future. You can use --format=legacy (or define a list_format in your pip.conf) to disable this warning.


CondaValueError: Value error: invalid package specification: None:///<unknown>::appscript=1.0.1=py27_0
```

I tried again after doing a `source activate root` and got the same result.  I then switched to a different environment and it worked perfectly (no deprecation warnings or anything).

I think trying to snapshot the root environment might be a common sort of operation for someone to perform, but perhaps not.  I don't understand why I sometimes had the `DEPRECATION` warning and sometimes not.

issue_title: invalid conda sub-commands do not report error
issue_body:
If I mis-type a conda sub-command I do not get an error message, I only get a non-zero exit code.

Say I wanted to do:

```
conda create -q -n myenv python=3.6 pandas
```

but instead I fat-fingered it and wrote:

```
conda creat -q -n myenv python=3.6 pandas
```

Or put that in a script.

If I checked the error code (`echo $?`) I'd see that the spelling mistake resulted in an exit code of 1.  But if I only look at the output, and don't see any (thanks to `-q` in this case) then I'd be surprised that the command hadn't actually done anything.

issue_title: Allow for environment specific configuration
issue_body:
It would be useful to allow for an environment specific confgiguration so that we can append configuration to the user/default condarc based configuration.

We run our CI using conventions so we know a project expects conda if there's a known file (conda.txt) present and we use that file to install the required packages for that application:

    conda create -p env --file conda.txt

however there's often times when someone needs a package from a custom channel. We were updating all our .condarc files to include that channel but have just implemented a solution that looks for a specific file* that we read in as additional arguments:

    CONDA_ARGS=""
    if [ -f conda.args ]; then
        CONDA_ARGS=`cat conda.args`
    fi
    conda create -p env --file conda.txt $CONDA_ARGS

the `conda.args` file is just as you would expect, a set of args to append to the command 

    -c foo -c bar -c baz

However i think it would be really useful to be able to append an additional configuration (in the condarc format) for an individual environment or command on the command line: 

    conda create -p env --file conda.txt --config conda.config

    conda config --env conda.config

issue_title: Basic authentication broken: login:password components from channel urls lost by prioritize_channels()
issue_body:
With conda 4.2.9,
running `conda search xxx`, or `conda install xxx` on a channel (`https://user:password@server/channels`) protected by basic authentication fails `with HTTPError: 401 Client Error: Unauthorized for url: https://server/channels/osx-64/repodata.json: https://server/channels/osx-64/`

For what I understand, `user:password` components are lost by 
```
def prioritize_channels(channels):
    # ('https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/osx-64/', ('conda-forge', 1))
    result = odict()
    for q, chn in enumerate(channels):
        channel = Channel(chn)
        for url in channel.urls:
            if url in result:
                continue
            result[url] = channel.canonical_name, q
```
where
```
channel._raw_value = https://user:password@server/channels
result = OrderedDict([(u'https://server/channels/osx-64/', (u'https://server/channels', 0)), (u'https://server/channels/noarch/', (u'https://server/channels', 0))])
```

issue_title: miniconda2 installs Python 3.5.2 when creating new env
issue_body:
# How to recreate the issue:

```bash
### Run the cflinuxfs2 Docker image
docker run -it cloudfoundry/cflinuxfs2 bash
### Install miniconda
wget https://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda2-4.1.11-Linux-x86_64.sh
chmod +x Miniconda2-4.1.11-Linux-x86_64.sh
./Miniconda2-4.1.11-Linux-x86_64.sh 
### Download environment.yaml file
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudfoundry/python-buildpack/develop/cf_spec/fixtures/miniconda_simple_app_python_2/environment.yml
### Create env 
conda env update -n dep_env -f environment.yml
### Activate env
source activate dep_env
### Check Python version
python --version
```

The python version appears as `Python 3.5.2 :: Continuum Analytics, Inc.` when we expect 2.7.11. We expect Python version 2 because we downloaded and installed miniconda 2. 

Note: we are not sure if this is a bug or a new feature. 

-- @RochesterinNYC

issue_title: Add a feature to pull unit_count for filters from index name
issue_body:
We are currently looking into building a flexible log management architecture that gives the log sender some control over how his log events are treated by processing metadata about that log source.
One of those fields is a time to live in days, so how long that event should be kept, before it is deleted.

Events are then stored in indices called logstash-30-yyyy.mm.dd (for a 30 day ttl) or logstash-60-yyyy.mm.dd (for a 60 day ttl) etc.
In order to enable housekeeping on these we'd need to create an action in curator per existing value for ttl (unless I am missing something), which is not desireable.
Would it be of interest to add functionality to specify a regex or something similar to enable curator to dynamically pull the value for unit_count from the index name? I'd be happy to look into this and come up with a proposal of how configuration could look first and work on a PR later on if people think this makes sense.

issue_title: Ability to Configure Azure CLI Trusted Root CAs
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

For an Azure Stack install, there is a requirement to add a Root CA for access via the Azure CLI. However, it is not currently possible to configure a custom CA for the Azure CLI toolset, and the Azure Stack documentation requires you to manually update the CA list in the library used by the Azure CLI toolset.

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-stack/user/azure-stack-version-profiles-azurecli2

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Replacing the CA list in the library is problematic, as upgrading Azure CLI toolset will wipe out the trusted CA list. It would be ideal to have a version-neutral configuration for adding trusted root CAs.

issue_title: `show` commands in the CLI will fail with an exit code of 3 upon a missing resource(404).
issue_body:
---
Opening this issue for comments/question/searches regarding the new change.
This decision is to resolve issues like https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/5006 and https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/6442.

Currently in our CLI we have two main issues related to our `show` commands:
1. There is a huge **inconsistency** in the way in which our commands deal with a 404(missing resource) status code returned from a service call.
Some commands will swallow the error msg and exit successfully with no output and some will fail with the response received. This makes it difficult at times for users transitioning from one service call to another and creates confusion regarding the status of the resource in question.

2. There needs to be a simple way to do an '**existence check**' on a resource. We do not have many `exists` commands in the CLI and for most services, the way to determine existence is to make the same service call that we currently do with our `show` commands- this is usually a GET call on a resource url. This existence check is necessary at times for scripting purposes before a `create` command is used.

In response to these issues, we have decided on modifying all show commands to fail with an **exit code of 3** upon a 404 returned from a service call, as per the title. 

This gives users an easy way to query for existence by checking the exit code of a `show` command and also addresses the feedback/issues we've gotten regarding the commands exitting successfully upon a missing resource error as being unintuitive to use.

Currently, in our CLI, we use a few exit codes already for different means:
-`0` success/normal
-`1` generic error
-`2` parsing error, check input to command-line
We will document these, as well as the new use of `3` as an exit code with our changes.

We are planning on making this change in our next sprint; here's a link to an initial PR adding support for registering `show` commands in this way: https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/pull/6489/files

tagging @tjprescott @yugangw-msft @mayurid

issue_title: Measure the load performance of individual command model
issue_body:
Moving forward, more and more command modules will be introduced. The performance of `az` relies on the performance of individual command modules as well. Especially in the case of loading commands and parameters. If one command module regresses the performance, the overall performance will be bad.

We don't have a mechanism to measure individual command module's performance in place. We need one for the 3rd party contributors to validate their performance as well as serves as gatekeeper for az cli.

issue_title: [Output] Remove "list" output type
issue_body: The "list" output type was abandon months ago in favor of the JSON output type, and while you cannot set list as a default output type with `az configure` you can still specify `-o list`. issue_title: pybullet transform debug tools
issue_body:
Two tools would be extremely helpful for pybullet:

 1. A way to add arbitrary poses/coordinate systems with optional visualization, like an unlimited version of CoodinateSystemDemo. 
 2. A useful companion would be a way to get arbitrary transforms between any two objects, including what is loaded from files like URDF. 

Is there a transform object with a vector + quaternion or are position and orientation always stored separately at the moment?

Here is my first pass at an API, which will need some refinement:

```python
import pybullet as p

# create/modify a pose object
id = p.setPoseObject(transform,
                     itemUniqueId=None # create by default
                     relativeToId=WorldFrameId, 
                     parentFrameId=WorldFrameId, 
                     ballOpacity=1, 
                     triadOpacity=1, 
                     name="PoseObject####",
                     color="yellow")

# move any object to an arbitrary position relative to an arbitrary frame
success = setTransform(itemUniqueId, 
                       transform, 
                       relativeToId=WorldFrameId)

# change the frame within which the specified frame moves
success = setParentFrame(itemUniqueId, parentUniqueId)

# get the id of the parent frame
parentId = getParent(itemUniqueId)

# get the transform between two arbitrary objects
transform = getTransform(itemUniqueId, relativeToId=WorldFrameId)

# get separate v,q (maybe not necessary if the transform above is [v,q]
v, q = getTransformVectorQuaternion(itemUniqueId, relativeToId=WorldFrameId)
```

It would be important that any frame moves along with its parent, for example if you were setting a tool point on a gripper, the frame should move with the gripper. If the world frame is the parent, it should remain static.

Why is this important? It will be extremely useful for debugging, defining goals and visualizing them, etc.

issue_title: Possible Memory Leak with SSL Sessions
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm calling S3 using:

-python 3.6.3
-aiobotocore 0.6.0
-Ubuntu 17.10

I notice that when I have a loop of async calls to retrieve data, my memory usage seems to continually go up, even though I expect it to stay the same.  Here is a test case I could come up with:

```python
import asyncio
from hashlib import md5
import aiobotocore
from botocore.client import Config
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError as BotoClientError
from itertools import islice, chain

class FileService(object):
    def __init__(self, loop=None):
        config = Config(signature_version='s3v4')
        self._loop = loop or asyncio.get_event_loop()

        self._session = boto3.Session()
        self._asession = aiobotocore.get_session(loop=self._loop)
        self._config = config

    def _get_s3_client(self):
        # Use the credentials created by boto3 session.
        creds = self._session.get_credentials()
        client = self._asession.create_client('s3', region_name="us-east-1",
                                              aws_secret_access_key=creds.secret_key,
                                              aws_access_key_id=creds.access_key,
                                              aws_session_token=creds.token,
                                              config=self._config)
        return client

    def readfiles(self, paths):
        return self._loop.run_until_complete(self.readfiles_async(paths))

    def readfile(self, path, client=None):
        return self._loop.run_until_complete(self.readfile_async(path, client=client))

    async def readfiles_async(self, paths):
        async with self._get_s3_client() as client:
            futures = [self.readfile_async(path, client=client) for path in paths]
            return await asyncio.gather(*futures)

    async def readfile_async(self, path, client=None):
        data = await self._get_object_async(client, path)
        return data

    async def _get_object_async(self, client, path):
        bucket, key = parse_s3_path(path)
        try:
            response = await client.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)
            async with response['Body'] as stream:
                return await stream.read()
        except BotoClientError as e:
            if e.response['Error']['Code'] == 'NoSuchKey':
                pass


def parse_s3_path(path):
    s3_tokens = path.split('/', 3)
    try:
        return s3_tokens[2], s3_tokens[3]
    except IndexError:
        return s3_tokens[2], ''


def chunked(iterable, size=None):
    if not size:
        yield iterable
    else:
        it = iter(iterable)
        while True:
            s = islice(it, size)
            yield chain([next(s)], s)


service = FileService()

ids = range(0, 10000)
for chunk in chunked(ids, 250):
    file_paths = ["s3://mybucket/%s.file" % x for x in chunk]
    all_data = service.readfiles(file_paths)
```

Each chunk increases the memory size my a noticeable margin, and does not seem to release it on subsequent chunks.

I am not an expert at debugging memory issues in python, but I tried using objgraph to see which objects were getting created on each subsequent loop and found that the majority appear to be SSL contexts.

Thanks,
Tim

I tried to set up a full node over the past couple days.

After installing core and letting it index, I ended up with an API endpoint pointing to node.blockstack.org. I tried changing the node.blockstack.org entry in `client.ini` to localhost, but that didn't work. Not sure if it's because another setting (https?) needs to be changed.

The current documentation doesn't provide step by step instructions that work without additional guessing or troubleshooting on the part of someone following the instructions.

issue_title: Incorrect unused import warning with `as`
issue_body:
If a package is imported with an alias, and a submodule of that package is imported separately, pyflakes does not understand when a use of the submodule occurs.

This is related to #137, but the opposite problem. Warnings are reported when they shouldn't.

Consider this code:

```python
import scipy as sp
import scipy.spatial.distance

sp.spatial.distance.cdist(
    [(1, 2), (3, 4)],
    [(5, 6), (7, 8)],
)
```

Pyflakes incorrectly claims that `scipy.spatial.distance` is unused, despite being used on line 4:

```
$ pyflakes < ~/Desktop/pyflakes_import.py 
<stdin>:2: 'scipy.spatial.distance' imported but unused
```

Details:
```
$ pyflakes --version
1.6.0
```

issue_title: If method process fails, the Exception is ignored
issue_body:
In https://github.com/androguard/androguard/blob/master/androguard/decompiler/dad/decompile.py#L261 the exception of the processed method is caught and never raised.
This causes problems, if some error occurs while decompiling. Then the resulting source will be broken.

It really depends on the analysis method, what you want in this situation.
For me, where I mostly dump the whole source, it is certainly good to know if an exception happens, so I can use other decompilation techniques.

I'm not really sure how to tackle that problem.
In my case, I "fixed" it by raising the exception and try other methods then, if the get_source() function of a Class fails.
I'm also not sure if it is wise to implement too many flags, like a nonstopmode, a raiseexception mode, ... and whatnotelse.

issue_title: We've experiencied a few messages lost
issue_body:
confluent-kafka==0.9.2, librdkafka 0.9.2, kafka 0.10.1.0, Python 2.7.12

We run an API and sending messages to Kafka, when we run the following code we've experienced some messages lost, we check offsets of kafka and the offsets is less than expected.
In any moment is raising or logged any error or exception.

```python

 self._client = Producer(config_)

try:
    self._client.produce(topic, message)
except BufferError:
    self._client.poll(0)
    self._client.produce(topic, message)
```
config used on producer 
```json
        "message.send.max.retries": 5,
        "retry.backoff.ms": 100,
        "queued.min.messages": 1
```
We could fix the error calling flush after every message sent, with result a massive lost of performance.

```python
try:
    self._client.produce(topic, message)
    self._client.flush()
except BufferError:
    self._client.poll(0)
    self._client.produce(topic, message)
```

Is The correct approach ? what is the best way to avoid messages lost and not lost performance ?


##new Information

The messages that was lost, are the last ones, the producer do not guarantee that all messages are sent.

The workaround that fixed it without lost of performance was the following approach
```
atexit.register(self._client.produce.flush)
```

Is The correct approach ?

issue_title: Fuzzy parse whas a problem with numbers in tokens.
issue_body:
This snippet:

```python
from dateutil.parser import parse
parse('04/04/04 +32423', fuzzy_with_tokens=True)
```

Throws a `ValueError`, despite the fact that it contains a valid date.
```
    455
    456         if res is None:
--> 457             raise ValueError("Unknown string format")
    458
    459         repl = {}

ValueError: Unknown string format
```

It seems to me that when doing fuzzy parsing, if you find *anything* that could be a date, incorporate that into the date, and anything that's not a date should be included as a token. I haven't looked too deeply into it, but I would guess that this is causing problems because of one of the lazy-evaluation shortcuts. It seems to happen whenever a number is introduced:

```python
from dateutil.parser import parse
parse('04/04/04 Dog470', fuzzy=True)
```

TB:
```
    456         if res is None:
--> 457             raise ValueError("Unknown string format")
    458
    459         repl = {}

ValueError: Unknown string format
```

issue_title: Extend Dict field type to allow specifying type of dictionary values
issue_body:
Currently there is a Dict field type that represents raw dictionary of any data. This container field type is similar to List field type, except for List allows specifying type of list elements while Dict type does not.

Proposal: add an argument to Dict to specify type of dict values (keys are always strings). This will allow to work with mappings of some values for which number and exact list of keys is not known.

item_features = pd.get_dummies(item_features, columns=['tag'])
item_features = item_features.T.to_dict().values()
vec = DictVectorizer(sparse=True)
item_features = vec.fit_transform(item_features)
```
I think the problems lie with `item_features = item_features.T.to_dict().values()` and `DictVectorizer` part?
The train set and test test shape is both (27393, 6676). And `item_features` shape is (6778, 6918). There are 241 distinct tags for item features.

issue_title: Strings that should not be argparsed as different python literals
issue_body:
Updating a string valued config parameter with e.g. "123" turns the string parameter into an integer. This might lead to unexpected behaviour. One could manually convert it back to a string, but it would be nicer if _sacred_ had a way to handle this automatically.
Unfortunately `python command with param=123` and `python command with param="123"` is treated equally because the python _argparse_ never actually sees the quotation marks.

Is there maybe a different work around or something I am forgetting?

issue_title: Overwriting list elements of a configuration not documented
issue_body:
Let us assume my process consists of a list of steps:

```
@experiment.config
def config():
    model_sequence = [
        dict(name='a', model_id='5943a94ba421dc1600fdb4e2'),
        dict(name='b'),
        dict(name=b'),
        dict(name='c', a_min=1e-6, a_max=1 - 1e-6),
        dict(name='evaluate')
    ]
```

How can I, for example overwrite `model_sequence[-2]['a_min']`?

It seems to be either not implemented or not documented well.

If you recommend a completely different approach to define such a setup, I am also welcoming it.

issue_title: Clean up modules
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Ben Ockmore (Bitbucket: [LordSputnik](http://bitbucket.org/LordSputnik), GitHub: [LordSputnik](http://github.com/LordSputnik))**

----------------------------------------

This issue is mainly for convenience, so that I can refer to it in edit notes instead of repeating myself all the time!

Off the top of my head, the remaining clean-up actions are as follows:

* Port any remaining meaningful improvements from mutagenx (only test modules remaining to go through)
* Make sure that all module headers start with the PEP 236 encoding string, followed by a blank line, then the names of copyright holders, in the format specified by the GPL.
* Ensure that all modules within the mutagen package have module docstrings to explain what they do.
* Fix all reasonable problems detected by Pylint.
* Add documentation for all public-facing functions and classes, where missing.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lazka/mutagen/issue/196

issue_title: make manager optional argument in HDF5IO
issue_body:
current use
```
from form.backends.hdf5 import HDF5IO
from pynwb import get_build_manager

manager = get_build_manager()
io = HDF5IO(filename, manager, mode='w')
io.write(f)
io.close()
```

this includes two lines that look like boilerplate:

```
from pynwb import get_build_manager

manager = get_build_manager()
```

this should be simplified to 

```
from form.backends.hdf5 import HDF5IO

io = HDF5IO(filename, mode='w')
io.write(f)
io.close()
```

while allowing the user to specify a different build manager if they want it

> print(qs.query)
> ```
> This is the query being generated:
> _SELECT "app_property"."id" FROM "app_property" WHERE ("app_property"."monthly_price" <= 1000 AND "app_property"."monthly_price_currency" = USD)_ 
> 
> **But if I do some subfiltering in the original queryset then it lost the currency comparison:**
> ```
> qs2 = Property.objects.filter(user=1)
> qs2 = qs2.filter(monthly_price__lte=MoneyPatched('1000', 'USD')).only('id')
> print(qs2.query)
> ```
> This is the query being generated:
> _SELECT "app_property"."id" FROM "app_property" WHERE ("app_property"."user_id" = 1 AND "app_property"."monthly_price" <= 1000)_ 
> 
> Am I doing anything wrong? 
> Based on some extended testing the currency comparison gets properly implemented only when the money filter is the first filter implemented in the Model.objects. query, otherwise the currency gets ignored.
>

issue_title: Bug in deserialization
issue_body:
It is a regression introduced in e9d02c546ffeb726ea43084ed4a8153d40c2a6f0.
Before that commit the `obj['fields']` included all fields and m2m relations like `user_permissions` and `groups` on the `User` model.
The commit removes M2M fields from deserialized data, which leads to data loss.

I'll submit PR with tests and fix.

issue_title: 关于stock_account.py->_handle_dividend_book_closure函数中的dividend_reinvestment
issue_body:
在stock_account.py中的197行:_handle_dividend_book_closure函数中的通过dividend_reinvestment模拟股票分红再投资, 其代码如下:

```
    def _handle_dividend_book_closure(self, trading_date):
...
            if config.extra.dividend_reinvestment:
                last_price = Environment.get_instance().data_proxy.get_bar(order_book_id, trading_date).close
                shares = position.quantity * dividend_per_share / last_price
                position._quantity += shares
            else:
...

```
在上述代码中, 分红再投资是通过用分红当日的**收盘价**购买股票来模拟的, 这不太准确的。
若是要模拟得像未除权一样, 应该是 **用昨日的未复权收盘价卖出该支股票, 然后将卖得的所有资金再用今日的复权后昨收盘价购买该支股票**, 这样获得的股票份数就是模拟出的未除权份数.

如果是用追加购买的方式来模拟, 上面的代码应该是用**复权后**的**昨收盘价**进行购买, 而不应该用当日的收盘价购买。

issue_title: Dirac job completion bug ( Ganga + GridPPDIRAC)
issue_body:
Some DIRAC jobs are grabbing the output sandbox which contains an (empty) ```__postprocesslocations__``` which overwrites the one generated from talking to DIRAC...

I am opening an issue because I'm going to take a look at the order in which jobs output sandbox and data is retrieved and to check that we don't overwrite _important_ files.

Ultimately we shouldn't be creating this file on the WN for ```DiracFile``` on a Dirac WN so there is a bug there which needs to be addressed and may fix this.
This code isn't called and just creates the file which isn't wanted.

I've manually verified the file exists for the job and has a correctly assigned JDL on the GridPP DIRAC after I fixed some other outstanding bugs to do with handling files/jobs.

issue_title: Hook to change the POSTed/PUTed json data
issue_body:
For Fennec, we need to convert the file that's uploaded via the kinto-admin and add some info.

Specifically, the "original" key you see here https://github.com/pocmo/Fennec-DLC-Catalog/blob/master/create_catalog.py#L136 is added to keep track of the original file, and the file itself is zipped.

The goal is to let users upload fonts file in kinto admin and let Kinto take care of all the gzipping work

To do this, I propose the addition of a `kinto.core.events.BeforeResourceChanged` event, that will let us manipulate the json data right before it's turned into a SQL record and pushed in the database (and before the file is pushed in S3 via the kinto-attachment plugin)

issue_title: s/lightweight/simple
issue_body:
Following up a discussion about the usage of the word "lightweight" with @leplatrem - I am suggesting removing it from the documentation and replacing it with "simple".

The word "lightweight" has too many different meanings - both positive and negative. Is it lightweight in terms of RAM usage ? of complexity of deployment ? of features ? etc..

Simple seems to me like a word that describes best what is intended with the Kinto **API** : trying to KISS.

issue_title: Migration plan for moving to the sqlalchemy-redshift org
issue_body:
There are some details we'll need to deal with when this project moves to the https://github.com/sqlalchemy-redshift org.

* Documentation
  * [ ] Make sure we're using sqlachemy-redshift and sqlalchemy_redshift consistently everywhere
  * [x] Make sure any references to the repo are correct
* [Read the Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/redshift-sqlalchemy)
  * [x] Change the url of docs to `sqlalchemy-redshift` (it's currently inverted)
  * [x] Set up a webhook on the repo so docs will build automatically.
* PyPI
  * [x] [Make redshift-sqlalchemy rely on sqlalchemy-redshift](https://github.com/binarydud/redshift_sqlalchemy/pull/33)

I wanted to make sure to document thoughts as I have them. Other things we'll need to keep in mind?

issue_title: CopyVaspOutputs and scratch_dir
issue_body:
I am using atomate and its default workflows to produce this sequence:
Static -> Uniform -> Boltztrap

I set a scratch_dir in the fw_worker.yaml.
What I get is that in the directory where the uniform task stores the final results,
I have a vasprun.xml that correspond to the static task and a vasprun.xml.gz that 
is the result of the uniform calculation. I think that is due to use of a scratch_dir.
The problem is that the CopyVaspOutputs inside the Boltztrap firetask copies 
by default the vasprun.xml (static calculation) and not the vasprun.xml.gz (unifom one)
that is what I want but in general what it should be copied.

issue_title: RuntimeError: No matching overloads found. at native/common/jp_method.cpp:117
issue_body:
I got this error when calling ```IdaDataBase(connection_string)```. I noticed that in file ```base.py``` line 284, ```self._connection_string``` is a Python list, and the subsequent call ```jaydebeapi.connect()``` raises error when reading ```self._connection_string``` as a list. My solution is to change ```self._connection_string``` to a string, in the format of ```'jdbc:db2://<HOST>:<PORT>/<DBNAME>:user=<UID>;password=<PWD>'```, and it works.

I encountered this error in Mac OS, Python 3.6, ibmdbpy version 0.1.4, JayDeBeApi 1.0.0, jpype 0.6.2, jdk 1.8. I wonder whether this is a bug, or there is the right way to resolve this?

issue_title: Test that rpms can be published to custom directory
issue_body:
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1976

Test that a separate directory can be specified where packages can be published.

Context
There was decision made in the past which led to this split (repodata and packages in separate directory) and that's why we have to keep this structure compatible. Although we have some repositories where repodata are in the same directory as packages. I don't know historical details, but as I mentioned above, fedora repositories also use this.

Acceptance Criteria
- attribute has to be optional
- attribute has to be in format that is allowed by mkdir
- if attribute is not set, packages have to be sync to relative_path of repository

issue_title: Question on perturb_vars function in Parameter Noise
issue_body:
baselines/deepq/build_graph.py  Line 225

```python
def perturb_vars(original_scope, perturbed_scope):
    all_vars = U.scope_vars(U.absolute_scope_name("q_func"))
    all_perturbed_vars = U.scope_vars(U.absolute_scope_name("perturbed_q_func"))
    assert len(all_vars) == len(all_perturbed_vars)
    perturb_ops = []
    for var, perturbed_var in zip(all_vars, all_perturbed_vars):
        if param_noise_filter_func(perturbed_var):
            # Perturb this variable.
            op = tf.assign(perturbed_var, var + tf.random_normal(shape=tf.shape(var), mean=0., stddev=param_noise_scale))
        else:
            # Do not perturb, just assign.
            op = tf.assign(perturbed_var, var)
        perturb_ops.append(op)
    assert len(perturb_ops) == len(all_vars)
    return tf.group(*perturb_ops)
```

`original_scope` and `perturbed_scope` are not used inside the function. However, two different function call can be found below, namely

Line 245
```python
perturb_for_adaption = perturb_vars(original_scope="q_func", perturbed_scope="adaptive_q_func")
```

Line 271
```python
tf.cond(reset_ph, lambda: perturb_vars(original_scope="q_func", perturbed_scope="perturbed_q_func"), lambda: tf.group(*[]))
```

I think that might be a clerical mistake, and you can easily fix it.

issue_title: Issue in pmag.dia_vgp
issue_body:
Hi devs, I was converting a direction at a given site to a VGP and then back at that same location to a direction expecting to get the same vector. Here is my code:

    site_latitude = 20
    site_longitude = -100
    dec = 40
    inc = 50

    vgp = dia_vgp(dec, inc, 1, site_latitude, site_longitude)
    dir = vgp_di(vgp[1], vgp[0], site_latitude, site_longitude)

    print dir
    >>> (320.0, 50.000000000000028)

This is unexpected and does match my original input. I think it is being caused by addition of 360 if the pole longitude is negative in dia_vgp:

    plong = plong+2*np.pi

if I comment out this line the result becomes:

    >>> (40.000000000000007, 50.000000000000014)

which is my original direction. So perhaps we should keep the returned pole longitude between -180 and 180 instead of 0 and 360. Let me know if I'm mistaken ..

Best and thanks!

issue_title: api_callable._bundleable will fail as it does not handle kwargs

issue_title: aloe does not run on win
issue_body:
# Aloe seems not to run on win32/win64 at all.

I would think it appropriate to at least mention this explicitly in the documentation.
## Steps to reproduce.
1. Clean install of python
2. `pip install virtualenv`
3. Create virtual environment
4. Activate virtual environment
5. `pip install aloe`
6. `aloe --help` produces the following stack trace
   
   ```
     (py3) C:\virtual_envs>aloe --help
     Traceback (most recent call last):
       File "c:\progs\python35\Lib\runpy.py", line 170, in _run_module_as_main
         "__main__", mod_spec)
       File "c:\progs\python35\Lib\runpy.py", line 85, in _run_code
         exec(code, run_globals)
       File "C:\virtual_envs\py3\Scripts\aloe.exe\__main__.py", line 9, in <module>
       File "c:\virtual_envs\py3\lib\site-packages\aloe\__init__.py", line 33, in main
         from aloe.runner import Runner
       File "c:\virtual_envs\py3\lib\site-packages\aloe\runner.py", line 17, in <module>
         from aloe.plugin import GherkinPlugin
       File "c:\virtual_envs\py3\lib\site-packages\aloe\plugin.py", line 24, in <module>
         from aloe.result import AloeTestResult
       File "c:\virtual_envs\py3\lib\site-packages\aloe\result.py", line 17, in <module>
         import blessings
       File "c:\virtual_envs\py3\lib\site-packages\blessings\__init__.py", line 5, in <module>
         import curses
       File "c:\progs\python35\Lib\curses\__init__.py", line 13, in <module>
         from _curses import *
     ImportError: No module named '_curses'
   ```

All other interactions I have tried so far--in particular running a feature file--produce the same result.
## System information

Operating System: Windows 10, Version 1511, Build 10586.545

```
> python
Python 3.5.1 (v3.5.1:37a07cee5969, Dec  6 2015, 01:54:25) [MSC v.1900 64 bit (AMD64)] on win32

> pip list 
aloe (0.1.8)
blessings (1.6)
future (0.15.2)
gherkin-official (4.0.0)
nose (1.3.7)
pip (8.1.2)
repoze.lru (0.6)
setuptools (26.1.1)
wheel (0.29.0)
```
## Dependency on `curses` via `blessings`

It seems to be well-known that `curses` is not supported on Windows builds of Python. `blessings` depends on `curses`. In the `blessings` project there is an open issue regarding this with long discussion. However the issue is quite old (2012) and seems not to move in any definitive direction.

[Issue 21: completly fails on win32/64](https://github.com/erikrose/blessings/issues/21)
## Suggestion/Questions
- Would it be possible to make the dependency on `blessings` optional? 
- Would it be possible to offer an option to use an alternative (e.g. colorama)

issue_title: Proposal: Ending Django 1.8, 1.9, 1.10 support
issue_body:
I'm proposing dropping Django versions older than 1.11 from a near-future Anymail update. (Django's 1.8 long-term-support period ended earlier this year, and Django 1.9 and 1.10 have been unsupported for a while.)

I would continue to release security fixes on the Anymail 2.x branch that supports Django 1.8, 1.9, and 1.10 through April, 2019. Also, if at all possible, Anymail 2.x would be patched if an ESP it supports makes a breaking change to their API.

But new Anymail features and new ESPs would only be available in Anymail 3.x with Django 1.11 or later.

If you're using Anymail with an older Django version and have any concerns about this, please comment.

issue_title: Feature request for exception translators
issue_body:
Pybind11 nicely converts exceptions derived from `std::exception` into corresponding Python exceptions. However it does not currently seem to support custom exceptions. In Boost Python this functionality is provided by exception translators (http://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_61_0/libs/python/doc/html/reference/high_level_components/boost_python_exception_translato.html).
What are your thoughts about adding equivalent functionality to pybind11?

issue_title: Interoperability with cython
issue_body:
I am sure I am not the only one, but I have historically developed a number of interlinking packages based on cython interfaces, cython headers, etc.

Having discovered pybind11, I am totally enamored.  I especially like how wrapping a class to python involves just a single C++ method definition if you are lucky, and you are good to go.  I've been doing lots of work with pybind11 recently.

I especially like how easy it is to drop into a visual studio/Xcode debugger inside the C++ code called from the python module.  That has drastically improved my compile-debug-compile cycle throughput.  Previously it was all just print commands in my cython code (yuck!)...

But the crux of my problem is that I would like to switch over my API to use pybind11 instead of cython while retaining the ability to make the module(s) interoperable with cython.   I know this is a big ask, but I thought I would put it out there as something I am very interested in and would like to know if there is any way to make it work.

import pytest

# Import Report Portal logger and handler to the test module.
from pytest_reportportal import RPLogger, RPLogHandler

def test_one():
    logger.info("Case 1. Step 1")
    x = "this"
    logger.info("x is: %s", x)
    assert 'h' in x

#     Message with an attachment.

issue_title: Certbot fails to get certificates under Ubuntu 16.04
Handle symlinked account deletion
issue_body:
From discussion at https://github.com/certbot/certbot/issues/6155.

> As a more minor point, we should probably check that symlinked accounts are deleted in the way we expect.

Deleting the accounts deletes the particular account under the `directory/` folder, which remains symlinked. If you then create an ACMEv2 account, it puts the ACMEv2 account into the ACMEv1 folder, with a symlink to it from ACMEv2.

The easiest way to deal with this is probably to delete directories if they're empty.

issue_title: Elasticsearch analyser Internal server error.
issue_body:
<!-- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
Internal server error caused on search as "pt_analyzer" is missing

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9539326/36855172-b9a53ffe-1d6a-11e8-9697-156ed61bc928.png)

## Detailed Description
uData is loooking for a specific Elasticsearch analyser depending on "DEFAULT_LANGUAGE" setting.
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/__init__.py#L113-L115

In our case as our default language setting is "pt" this custom analyser doesn't exist, causing an AttributeError:
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/analysis.py#L71 

## Possible Implementation
<!-- Not obligatory, but suggest an idea for implementing addition or change -->
In our case we will need also a custom analyser named "pt_analyzer" because we will need special Elasticsearch filters as for example the "ASCII Folding Token filter" or "asciifolding", but it would be nice to set a default analyser when the i18n custom is missing.
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/__init__.py#L113-L115

by changing line L115 to:
 `return getattr(analysis, '{0}_analyzer'.format(language)) or getattr(analysis, 'standard')`

Let me know if this change makes sense, and also if we could add a custom "pt_analyzer". 
Can we open a pull request? :)

Best!

issue_title: Save as optimized svg generates invalid svg output
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce bug:

1. Create new document in inkscape
2. Click the text tool
3. Write something, press return, write something, press return, write something
4. Select the three lines of text and set a new font and font-size
5. Save as optimized svg, and make sure the "group collapsing" option is unchecked, and the "create groups for similar attributes" is checked

The generated output svg has a structure like this:

<svg>
  <text>
    <g>
      <tspan>...</tspan>
      <tspan>...</tspan>
      <tspan>...</tspan>
    </g>
  </text>
</svg>

The g tag in this case makes browsers not render the tspans. It's also invalid svg markup, since <g> isn't allowed as a child of <text>.

issue_title: add control panel to view and manipulate redis queue
ReversibleField performs in-place transpose operation
issue_body:
I am using the recently added `ReversibleField` field.
Converting padded indices into original words was always a cumbersome chore, and the newly added class greatly reduces this burden.
However, I found a strange behavior of the class; when `batch_first` is `True`, it performs in-place transpose operation, which I think not intuitive.
Is it an intended side effect?
The following is the code for reproducing this behavior.
```
TEXT = data.ReversibleField()
LABEL = data.Field(sequential=False)
train, dev, test = datasets.SNLI.splits(TEXT, LABEL)
TEXT.build_vocab(train, max_size=1000) 
LABEL.build_vocab(train) 
train_iter, dev_iter, test_iter = data.BucketIterator.splits((train, dev, test), batch_size=4)
b = next(iter(train_iter))
b.premise.size()  # torch.Size([7, 4])
TEXT.reverse(b.premise.data)
b.premise.size()  # torch.Size([4, 7])
```

And it is also problematic in that calling `TEXT.reverse(b.premise.data)` repeatedly does not give consistent results.
For example, when I call it for the first time, it works nicely:
```
['A girl jumps off of a diving board into a pool.',
 'The mountain biker is UNK the hill on a UNK trail.',
 'A man is standing next to a large black UNK statue.',
 'A young gentleman is pointing to a sign on a building.']
```
However if I call it again, since `b.premise.data` is transposed, it emits wrong results.
```
['A The A A',
 'girl mountain man young',
 'jumps biker is gentleman',
 'off is standing is',
 'of UNK next pointing',
 'a the to to',
 'diving hill a a',
 'board on large sign',
 'into a black on',
 'a UNK UNK a',
 'pool trail statue building',
 '....']
```

issue_title: [Suggestion] Regarding ImageFolder
issue_body:
The current ImageFolder is much too restrictive, as is evident by the number of PRs trying to add various functionalities.

Would it make sense to simply have find_classes [1] be a callable argument similar to how a custom loader can be specified? ImageFolder can then be made recursive without impacting the current functionality, and both existing PRs (#100, #47) would be special cases of this. This change would allow ImageFolder to be a much more versatile image loader.

[1] https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/datasets/folder.py#L17

issue_title: Centralise documentation for the output widget
issue_body:
Currently, documentation for the output widget is scattered across:
 - [async widgets](https://ipywidgets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples/Widget%20Asynchronous.html#Interacting-with-output-widgets-from-background-threads)
 - [widget list](https://ipywidgets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples/Widget%20List.html#Output)

With PR #1934 , we need to extend the docs for the output widget to expose its use as a decorator. 

Given the output widget's power, we think it makes sense to bring docs on the output widget to the top level. I think it would be useful to structure the notebook as:

 - introduction (what's currently in the widget list)
 - displaying rich output (e.g., with an example from matplotlib?)
 - async widgets
 - using the `@capture` decorator, including as a way to debug widget callbacks.

Happy to tackle this today / this weekend, unless someone else is particularly keen to.

---

Jason deserves the credit for the idea behind this issue.

issue_title: Allow cancelling individual tasks
issue_body:
I am trying to write a server with trio. The main loop looks roughly like this:

async def accept_loop(nursery, sock, context):
    '''Accept connections and start tasks to handle them'''

    while True:
        (client_sock, addr) = await sock.accept()
        proxy = ProxyServer(client_sock, context)
        nursery.start_soon(proxy.process_requests)

Now, I want to be able to shutdown the server cleanly. I could emit a cancel signal for the nursery,  but that wouldn't give the worker tasks a chance to finish processing the hcurrent request. Therefore, the workers check `proxy.should_terminate` after each request. By setting this flag and waiting a little, I can cleanly shut down the workers.

That leaves me with the problem of the stopping accept_loop(). I can't rely on a flag here, because sock.accept may block for a very long time. So I need to cancel this task. But if I do so while the worker tasks are still running, they will get terminated as well. And if I do it after the workers have shutdown, then I will keep accepting requests when I actually want to shut down.

It would make my life much easier if I could cancel a specific task, rather than the entire thread group. Is there a reason why `nursery.start_soon`  doesn't return something like curio's `Task` object which I can then use to cancel just this task?

@App('bash', dfk)

    with open('somefile.txt', 'w') as f:
        f.write("%s\n" % inputs[0])          #<--------------- Here inputs[0] is a DataFuture
    cmd_line = '''echo '{inputs[0]}' > {outputs[0]}'''
app1_future = app1(inputs = [],
                       outputs = [ "simple-out.txt"])
#app1_future.result()                                                                                                                                                    

app2_future = app2(inputs=[app1_future.outputs[0]],
                       outputs = ["simple-out2.txt"])
app2_future.result()

    return self.__deserialize_model(data, klass)
  File "/Users/user/virtualenvs/client4.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/kubernetes/client/api_client.py", line 622, in __deserialize_model
    instance = klass(**kwargs)
  File "/Users/user/virtualenvs/client4.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/kubernetes/client/models/v1_api_resource.py", line 82, in __init__
    self.singular_name = singular_name
